question: When testing a Zeebe gRPC call from the command line with `grpcurl`, which proto buffer file from the zeebe.io repository do you import to invoke methods such as `gateway_protocol.Gateway/Topology`?

a.text: The docs fetch `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/camunda/zeebe/main/zeebe/gateway-protocol/src/main/proto/gateway.proto` and use it via `-import-path /tmp -proto /tmp/gateway.proto` to call `gateway_protocol.Gateway/Topology`.

b.text: There is no `zeebe.proto` referenced — the gRPC contract lives under `gateway-protocol/src/main/proto/gateway.proto`.

c.text: `zeebe-broker.proto` is not a real file in the documented gRPC handling steps; the broker is reached through the gateway proto.

d.text: `Topology` is the gRPC method (`gateway_protocol.Gateway/Topology`), not the proto file name.

explanation: Camunda's gRPC handling guide pulls one proto file — `gateway.proto` from the zeebe-gateway-protocol path of the camunda/zeebe repo — and passes it to `grpcurl` with `-proto /tmp/gateway.proto`. The fully-qualified method `gateway_protocol.Gateway/Topology` lives inside that proto.
